metadata.purpose: Pre-coding mapping for Broker reference data - ADR-013

api_contract.description: Returns list of valid broker names for use in select dropdowns

value.description: Broker name value (singular, used as form value)

label.description: Broker display label (for UI, can be same as value)

forms_using_broker_select.notes: Required field - must select from list

implementation_notes.select_population: Select options will be populated dynamically from GET /api/v1/reference/brokers endpoint
implementation_notes.value_mapping: Form will submit 'value' field from API response
implementation_notes.display_mapping: UI will display 'label' field from API response
implementation_notes.fallback_behavior: If API fails, form must display an error message to the user. No fallback to manual text input is allowed. The select field must remain disabled until the API call succeeds. This ensures compliance with ADR-013: broker list source must be API-based only.
implementation_notes.caching: Consider caching broker list in frontend (sessionStorage or memory) to reduce API calls and improve performance. Cache should be invalidated on page refresh or when explicitly needed.

valid_brokers_list.notes: This is an initial reference list. The actual API endpoint may return a different set based on backend implementation (e.g., distinct values from brokers_fees table, or a dedicated reference table).

team_20_responsibilities: Define response schema matching the contract above
Determine data source (distinct from brokers_fees table, or dedicated reference table)
Ensure authentication is required (JWT Bearer token)
Return brokers in consistent format (value/label structure)

data_source_options.description: SELECT DISTINCT broker FROM user_data.brokers_fees WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
data_source_options.pros: Uses existing data, reflects actual brokers in use
data_source_options.cons: May not include all valid brokers, depends on user data

data_source_options.description: Create user_data.brokers_reference table with predefined list
data_source_options.pros: Complete list, consistent across users, can be maintained

data_source_options.description: Reference table + UNION with distinct from brokers_fees

backend_implementation_guidance.recommendation: Start with distinct_from_brokers_fees for MVP, consider reference table for future enhancement

team_30_responsibilities: Replace text input with select dropdown in D16 Trading Accounts form
Replace text input with select dropdown in D18 Brokers Fees form
Fetch broker list from GET /api/v1/reference/brokers on form load
Map API response (value/label) to select options
Implement error handling for API failures

select_implementation.empty_option: Include empty option for D16 (optional field): <option value=''>-- בחר ברוקר --</option>
select_implementation.loading_state: Show loading indicator while fetching broker list
select_implementation.error_state: Show error message if API call fails. Do not allow fallback to text input. Select field must remain disabled until API call succeeds. Display user-friendly error message explaining that broker selection is temporarily unavailable.

compliance.phoenix_bible_alignment: Field names follow Singular naming convention (broker, not brokers)
compliance.api_naming: snake_case for API fields (value, label), camelCase for frontend (value, label)
